title: Assert failure at startup using x86_64-w64-mingw32-g++ on Ubuntu 18.04
body:
Yosys silently fails an assert and exits without printing anything when built using `x86_64-w64-mingw32-g++` on Ubuntu 18.04.

When built with debug symbols and run under `gdb.exe`, I can place a breakpoint on `_exit` and get the following backtrace:

```
Breakpoint 2, 0x00000000007c9ec8 in _exit ()
(gdb) bt
#0  0x00000000007c9ec8 in _exit ()
#1  0x000000000043ae32 in Yosys::logv_error_with_prefix(char const*, char const*, char*) () at /usr/share/mingw-w64/include/process.h:46
#2  0x000000000043ae49 in Yosys::logv_error(char const*, char*) () at kernel/log.cc:343
#3  0x000000000043ae6c in Yosys::log_error(char const*, ...) () at kernel/log.cc:392
#4  0x0000000000404de9 in Yosys::log_assert_worker () at ./kernel/log.h:153
#5  0x00000000007e37fd in Yosys::RTLIL::IdString::get_reference(char const*) () at ./kernel/rtlil.h:148
#6  0x00000000007e4520 in _ZN5Yosys5RTLIL8IdStringC4EPKc () at ./kernel/rtlil.h:258
#7  0x00000000008fdbdf in _GLOBAL__sub_I_opt_share.cc () at ./kernel/rtlil.h:258
#8  0x00000000007ba072 in __do_global_ctors ()
#9  0x00000000004013cb in __tmainCRTStartup ()
#10 0x000000000040151b in mainCRTStartup ()
(gdb)
```

Removing the assert fixes the issue, however that's obviously not a good solution.

There are two issues here:

1. The assert is failing, and I don't know why.
2. The program is silently exiting without printing anything, probably because it's exiting before the console output has been set up.
